response.evidence: Statistical analysis reveals significant disparities in loan outcomes across racial groups: Asian applicants have the highest acceptance rate (74.7%) and receive the largest average loan amounts ($609,099), while White applicants show a 72.9% acceptance rate with lower average loans ($417,554), and Race Not Available applicants have notably lower acceptance rates (66.3%) with the smallest average loans ($432,503). Asian applicants also demonstrate higher average income ($265,421) and property values ($1,191,040) compared to other groups, suggesting differences in applicant profiles. However, when controlling for applicant characteristics (income and property value), the acceptance rate differential between groups remains substantial and unexplained by these factors alone, indicating potential disparate treatment in lending decisions.

Statistical analysis reveals disparities in loan outcomes across racial groups: Asian applicants have the highest acceptance rate (74.7%) and receive the largest average loan amounts ($609,099), while White applicants have a 72.9% acceptance rate with lower average loans ($417,555), and Race Not Available applicants have the lowest acceptance rate (66.3%) with the smallest average loans ($432,503). Asian applicants also have higher average income ($265.4k vs $244.9k and $223.5k for others) and property values ($1.19M vs $979k and $933k), which are legitimate lending factors. However, even accounting for these differences, the 8.4 percentage point gap between the lowest (66.3%) and highest (74.7%) acceptance rates, combined with systematic differences in loan amounts, suggests potential disparate treatment. The income and property value differences do not fully explain the acceptance rate variance.

Statistical disparities exist across groups: Asian applicants have the highest acceptance rate (74.70%), followed by White applicants (72.94%), while 'Race Not Available' group has notably lower acceptance rate (66.34%). Asian applicants also receive substantially higher average loan amounts ($609,099 vs $417,554 for White and $432,503 for Race Not Available). Asian applicants have higher average income ($265.42K vs $244.86K and $223.53K) and property values ($1.191M vs $979K and $933K). These consistent directional differences across multiple metrics suggest potential disparate treatment, though differences in applicant characteristics (income, property value) partially explain outcome variations. The acceptance rate gap of ~8 percentage points between highest and lowest groups warrants investigation into underwriting criteria.
